mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.16% is near zero, indicating an accommodative monetary stance. A sustained period without rate increases signals continued monetary ease, which depresses long-term Treasury yields as investors expect low real rates to persist. The 10-year yield falls as the market prices in extended accommodation.
regime_hint: monetary accommodation at zero lower bound
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 15 basis points over the 20-day window despite FEDFUNDS remaining below 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate at 7.2% signals slack labor market conditions. Elevated unemployment constrains wage growth and aggregate demand, reducing inflation expectations. Lower expected inflation compresses long-term Treasury yields as the market reprices terminal real rates downward over the 20-day horizon.
regime_hint: post-shock labor market recovery with persistent slack
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 10 basis points in instances where UNRATE > 7.0%, or the mechanism fails to predict yield direction in > 40% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) at 343,242 million dollars and housing starts (HOUST) at 625 thousand units jointly signal weak aggregate demand. When both demand proxies are below their 12-month moving averages simultaneously, the market reprices growth expectations downward, causing long-term Treasury yields to fall as investors shift to safe-haven positioning.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST decline > 5% from 3-month average
regime_hint: demand-constrained expansion with synchronized weakness across consumption and housing
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 50% of instances where both RSAFS and HOUST decline simultaneously, or the joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 106.1173 is near trend. A sustained 3-month decline in INDPRO signals supply-side weakness and potential stagflation risk. The market reprices inflation expectations upward and growth expectations downward, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as the term premium expands to compensate for stagflation uncertainty.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 20 basis points over 60 trading days despite INDPRO declining > 2%, or the mechanism fails to predict yield direction in > 45% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 135,489 thousand and the unemployment rate (UNRATE) at 7.2% jointly indicate a labor market in early recovery with persistent slack. When payroll growth accelerates while unemployment remains elevated, the market interprets this as non-inflationary growth, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall as inflation expectations remain anchored.
regime_hint: early-cycle recovery with labor market slack
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 55% of instances where PAYEMS growth accelerates with elevated UNRATE, or interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: CPI (CPIAUCSL) at 213.06 combined with FEDFUNDS at 0.16% reveals a large negative real rate. If inflation remains sticky above 2% while nominal rates stay near zero, the market reprices long-term inflation expectations upward, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as investors demand higher compensation for inflation risk.
regime_hint: inflation persistence with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls in > 50% of instances where inflation accelerates with near-zero rates, or the interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) at 11,712.4 billion chained 2017 dollars is the broadest measure of aggregate demand. A sustained quarterly decline in GDPC1 signals recession risk, causing the market to reprice long-term growth and inflation expectations downward, which compresses 10-year Treasury yields as investors flee to safe assets.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 25 basis points over 60 trading days following a quarterly GDP decline > 0.5%, or the mechanism fails to predict yield direction in > 40% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 106.1173 and CPI (CPIAUCSL) at 213.06 jointly signal supply-side constraints when INDPRO growth slows while inflation accelerates. This stagflation signal causes 10-year Treasury yields to rise as the market reprices both inflation risk and growth uncertainty, demanding higher term premiums.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < 0% AND CPIAUCSL month-over-month change > 0.3%
regime_hint: stagflation regime with simultaneous supply weakness and price acceleration
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls in > 50% of instances where INDPRO declines and CPI accelerates simultaneously, or the interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year window.
